column1: # CHANGES vs v1 (2026-06-12) — triage against the four laptop fits' evidence:
#   - REFERENCE is now C_m2nu4 (baseline_registry.md §2.1), accepted under the
#     RELAXED reference gate. CANDIDATES still face the STRICT §4 gate
#   - All formulas now modify the C_m2nu4 BASE (not m3): delay_seconds ~
#     + (1|route_id); student() with nu=constant(4); exp(0.5) on class=sd.
#   - High-cardinality main random INTERCEPTS on trip_id or stop_id are off the
#     table on the current 9-dense-day split (m3, C0_notrip, C0_nu4 all dirty;
#     C_m2nu4 only became near-clean once both were dropped). Candidates that
#     wanted "stop or trip structure" are reshaped to NON-funneling forms
#     (route-level random slopes, etc.) or dropped with the reason recorded.
#   - Queue order reflects cost vs hypothesis value: weather and bunching first
#     (cheap, high-signal, real hypotheses); structure climbs (C5) next;
#     last (expensive). Loop is preemption-safe — run_log.csv is the memory.
# The loop fits the LOWEST-numbered id with no run_log row. STOP when none
# remain or after 3 consecutive G4 non-passes (model_loop_spec.md §3).
# ── COMPLETED rows in exports/run_log.csv (kept here as audit trail) ──

column3: [2026-06-14, structural diagnostic] m3 minus (1|trip_id). R-hat 1.028, 42 div, ESS 147 → FAIL strict; dropping trip RE alone made things WORSE, ruling out single-RE-funnel as the m3 culprit.

[2026-06-15, likelihood experiment] m3 with nu fixed=4. R-hat 1.0114, 25 div, ESS 418 → FAIL strict (barely); nu-fix is necessary but not sufficient, and Step 1 localization showed remaining divergences are diffuse.

[2026-06-16, REFERENCE] m3 minus trip+stop REs, nu=4, exp(0.5) sd prior. R-hat 1.0043, 0.73% div, ESS 703 → near-clean. Accepted as REFERENCE under the relaxed gate (registry §2.1). All candidates below modify THIS base.

column1: # ── ACTIVE QUEUE — runs in this order on the cloud loop ──

column3: + precip_mm + temp_c (ECCC, joined from exports/weather_hourly.parquet) — rain raises delays beyond schedule features. Two continuous fixed effects, no new REs. HIGHEST hypothesis value (only tier-c covariate, fetcher already built).
+ log(headway_obs / headway_sched) — bunching amplifies delay beyond per-trip carry-forward. One continuous covariate (derived from stop_delays via SQL window); no new REs.

(1|route_id) → (1 + previous_stop_delay | route_id) — propagation strength varies by route (express vs local). 25-route grouping, NOT high-cardinality; this is the "structure without funneling" template, replaces the dropped-trip/stop REs.

+ timepoint * previous_stop_delay — drivers hold at timepoints; delay propagation should reset there (slope < off-timepoint slope). Single interaction, no new REs; timepoint joined from static stop_times.
shape_dist_traveled → s(shape_dist_traveled, bs="tp", k=8) — delay accumulation along the route is non-linear (recovery padding at termini). Smooth replaces linear term, no new REs.

TRAIN filtered to "settled" rows (actual_arrival ≤ timestamp) — VALIDITY PROBE: does b_prev fall meaningfully below 1 when the response is observation-grade rather than feed prediction? G4 not expected to pass; the finding IS the answer (reported regardless).
sigma ~ is_rush_hour + s(hour, bs="cc", k=8) — residual scale is time-varying (heteroscedastic). Distributional regression on σ only; nu stays fixed=4. ELPD/coverage-oriented, not RMSE.

[reshape, scale-up] same C_m2nu4 base, training scaled to top-50 routes × 1,500 rows (~75k) — does more data identify what 25×500 cannot (tighter posteriors, better held-out ELPD)? EXPENSIVE (~6-8h cloud); run LAST.

column1: # ── DROPPED (recorded for audit; lines commented out so the launcher skips) ──

column3: DROPPED 2026-06-16: m3 at adapt_delta=0.99. C0_nu4 at adapt_delta=0.95 with nu off the lb=2 floor (already better geometry than C0's plan) still produced 25 div / R-hat 1.0114. Sampler-tuning alone is insufficient against the m3 RE stack on 9 dense days. Superseded by the C_m2nu4 reference.

DROPPED 2026-06-16: ar(stop_sequence, gr=trip_serv_id, p=1). Within-trip AR(1) requires sequential observations inside each ~5,500 trip-day group; the frozen TRAIN is a random 500-rows-per-route subsample (~2 obs/trip-day on average) — the AR coefficient is not identifiable on this split. Not a funneling problem; a sampling-policy problem. Defer to a v2 split with by-trip stratification once the dataset fattens.
